description: Calico tier RBAC authorization bypass: apiserver only checks old tier, webhook only checks new tier on policy UPDATE operations

authorization_gap.apiserver_path: Update() in storage.go checks authorization against the OLD tier only — does not resolve the new object to check the destination tier
authorization_gap.webhook_path: authorize() in rbac.go checks the NEW object's tier on UPDATE — does not parse OldObject to check the source tier
authorization_gap.bypass_scenario: User with permission on tier A but not tier B can move a policy from tier A to tier B: apiserver allows (checks old=A, user has permission), webhook allows (checks new=B... but actually webhook checked new tier only, so if user has new tier permission it passes). The real gap: apiserver checks old tier only, webhook checks new tier only — neither checks BOTH tiers.

remediation: Apiserver Update() must call objInfo.UpdatedObject() to resolve the new object and check the new tier if it differs from the old
Webhook authorize() must parse request.OldObject on UPDATE operations and check the old tier if it differs from the new
Both paths must authorize against BOTH old and new tiers on tier-changing updates
